name: Verde (Spatial Interpolation and Gridding)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Fatiando a Terra / Leonardo Uieda

performance.notes: NumPy/SciPy-based; cross-validation adds overhead; scales to ~100K points

agent_readiness.notes: Scikit-learn-style spatial interpolation and gridding. import verde as vd. spline = vd.ScipyGridder(method='cubic'). spline.fit(coordinates=(lon, lat), data=values). grid = spline.grid(region=(-90, -70, -40, -20), spacing=0.5). pred = spline.predict(coordinates=(lon, lat)). ScipyGridder, Spline, VectorSpline2D, KNeighbors. Cross-validation: vd.cross_val_score(spline, coords, data). BlockMean for data decimation. Mask: vd.distance_mask. Pipeline: vd.Chain([trend, spline]).

gotchas: Coordinate convention: (longitude, latitude) as tuple, not (lat, lon)
Grid output is xarray Dataset — requires xarray knowledge
best_when: Spatial gridding of scattered point measurements
When scikit-learn-style API for spatial interpolation is needed
avoid_when: Large-scale production gridding (use kriging from specialized tools)
summary: Verde provides scikit-learn-style spatial interpolation and gridding for geoscience. vd.ScipyGridder().fit(coordinates=(lon, lat), data=values).grid(region=...). vd.cross_val_score(). BlockMean for decimation. vd.Chain for pipelines. BSD-3 by Fatiando. Best for geophysical field interpolation (gravity, magnetics) and scattered point gridding. Coordinate order: (lon, lat) tuples. Grid output is xarray Dataset.
